title: Git Troubleshooting Lab
description: Capstone lab for diagnosing detached HEAD, merge conflicts, lost commits, diverged branches, and remote sync issues.

tasks.prompt: Check repository status to identify unmerged paths and branch state.
tasks.explanation: git status is the first command in most Git incident triage.

tasks.prompt: Create branch 'recovery' from detached HEAD and switch to it.
tasks.hint: Use git switch -c recovery or checkout -b.
tasks.explanation: Creating a branch preserves work made in detached HEAD state.

tasks.prompt: List files still in conflict after a failed merge.
tasks.explanation: Unmerged paths must be identified before conflict resolution.

tasks.prompt: Abort the broken merge and return to the pre-merge branch tip.
tasks.explanation: Aborting restores a known-good state when a merge goes wrong.

tasks.prompt: Inspect reflog to locate the commit before the accidental reset.
tasks.explanation: reflog is the primary recovery tool for 'lost' local commits.

tasks.prompt: Hard reset the current branch to reflog entry HEAD@{1}.
tasks.explanation: Resetting to a reflog entry restores a previous branch position.

tasks.prompt: Fetch from origin and prune deleted remote-tracking branches.
tasks.explanation: Pruning removes stale remote references that cause confusion.

tasks.prompt: Show commits reachable from HEAD but not from origin/main.
tasks.explanation: This range reveals local commits not yet on the remote.

tasks.prompt: Abort a conflicted rebase in progress.
tasks.explanation: Aborting a rebase returns the branch to its pre-rebase state.

tasks.prompt: Stash uncommitted changes with message 'pre-hotfix backup'.
tasks.hint: Use git stash push -m with a descriptive message.
tasks.explanation: Stashing protects work in progress before emergency branch switches.

tasks.prompt: Cherry-pick commit 'f1x1234' onto the current hotfix branch.
tasks.explanation: Cherry-pick brings a single fix commit onto another branch.

tasks.prompt: Force-push the current branch to origin safely using --force-with-lease.
tasks.explanation: --force-with-lease refuses to overwrite remote commits you have not seen.
